title: roasted viscous ukrainian courgettes bowl
description: a lacto-vegetarian-friendly roasted courgettes dish perfect for a solo dinner, served in a korean brass bowl.
instructions: cut the courgettes into 1-inch pieces and place them in a bowl.
drizzle the olive oil over the courgettes and sprinkle with salt, black pepper, and paprika.
toss the courgettes to coat evenly and spread them on a baking sheet.
roast the courgettes in the preheated oven for 20 minutes.
while the courgettes are roasting, mix the sour cream, chopped parsley, lemon juice, and chopped walnuts in a bowl.
add the roasted courgettes to the bowl and toss with the sour cream mixture.
sprinkle the grated cheddar cheese, pickled mustard seeds, and chopped chives over the top.
serve the roasted courgettes in a korean brass bowl, garnished with chopped parsley and lemon wedges.
if desired, drizzle with vinaigrette and sprinkle with dried dill weed and sugar.
if desired, top with chopped walnuts and grated cheddar cheese.
reheat leftovers in the oven at 35056f (180�bc) for about 10 minutes, or until heated through.
adjust seasoning as needed, then serve hot, topped with extra parsley and lemon wedges if desired.
for an added crunch, sprinkle some chopped walnuts or chopped fresh chives on top.
for a creamier sauce, add more sour cream or substitute with plain yogurt.
for a tangier sauce, add more lemon juice or substitute with apple cider vinegar.
